briefdescription.para: the user can directly manipulate the vector, anyway she cannot break anything

doubles reserved memory until resize(newSize, value) can be done without reallocation Generated from method MR.Vector<MR.Vector<MR.ICPGroupPairs, MR.Id<MR.ICPElemtTag>>, MR.Id<MR.ICPElemtTag>>::resizeWithReserve.

sets elements [pos, pos+len) to the given value, adjusting the size of the vector to include new elements; the elements in between old size and pos are also set to val (for faster implementation) Generated from method MR.Vector<MR.Vector<MR.ICPGroupPairs, MR.Id<MR.ICPElemtTag>>, MR.Id<MR.ICPElemtTag>>::autoResizeSet.

sets the element #i to the given value, adjusting the size of the vector to include new element Generated from method MR.Vector<MR.Vector<MR.ICPGroupPairs, MR.Id<MR.ICPElemtTag>>, MR.Id<MR.ICPElemtTag>>::autoResizeSet.
this accessor automatically adjusts the size of the vector Generated from method MR.Vector<MR.Vector<MR.ICPGroupPairs, MR.Id<MR.ICPElemtTag>>, MR.Id<MR.ICPElemtTag>>::autoResizeAt.

std.vector<T>-like container that requires specific indexing type,

parameterdescription.para: type of index (shall be convertible to size_t) Generated from class MR.Vector<MR.Vector<MR.ICPGroupPairs, MR.Id<MR.ICPElemtTag>>, MR.Id<MR.ICPElemtTag>>. This is the non-const half of the class.
